name: MMDrive
finding: VERIFIED UNRELEASED as of 2026-06. The official repo (github.com/ziiho08/MMDrive) loads but contains ONLY LICENSE + README.md (confirmed via GitHub Contents API). No releases published. README verbatim: "## Updates [2025/04] Repository opened. The dataset will be released soon. Stay tuned! ## Access and Usage" — the Access and Usage section is an empty header with no content. GitHub API shows last code push 2025-04-11 (pushed_at), only 3 commits ever (Initial commit, Create README, Update README — all 2025-04-11), repo size 3 KB, no commits/updates beyond April 2025. There is NO download URL, form, email request, Google Drive, or HuggingFace link anywhere. The prior researcher's claim is fully confirmed. Additional corroboration: there is an OPEN, UNANSWERED issue # (titled "Data release", created 2026-02-11) from another researcher asking "when and how the public can access the dataset" — still open with no maintainer reply, confirming the data was still not available in early 2026. Not gated behind a form/agreement either — there is simply no access mechanism at all yet. CONCRETE NEXT ACTION: There is no way to obtain the bytes right now. Options: (1) Watch/star the repo and monitor for a new dated entry under "## Updates" or a populated "## Access and Usage" section; (2) Email the corresponding author of the CVPRW 2025 ABAW paper (Choi et al., "MMDrive: Multi-modal Remote Physiological Signal Measurement Dataset for Driver Status") to request access directly — the paper PDF (openaccess.thecvf.com/content/CVPR2025W/ABAW/papers/Choi_MMDrive_..._paper.pdf) lists author affiliations/contact; (3) Comment on / +1 the existing open GitHub issue about data release to signal demand. Do NOT expect a download today.

name: UBFC-rPPG (Dataset 2, 42 subjects)
finding: VERIFIED downloadable now (mid-2026), not gated. The official Google Sites page (HTTP 200) links a PUBLIC Google Drive folder (https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1o0XU4gTIo46YfwaWjIgbtCncc-oF44Xk) that opens without any access-request prompt and contains DATASET_1/, DATASET_2/ (the 42-subject realistic dataset), reader scripts (ubfcrppg_data_processor.m/.py), and a readme.txt. An Agreement.xlsx (10KB, 2018) sits in the folder but does NOT block access — no form/login/signed-and-returned agreement is needed; the only stated requirement is to cite the 2017 Bobbia et al. paper. So the prior claim is essentially right but is better described as a DIRECT public Drive download, not 'open_form_or_request'; emailing yannick.benezeth@u-bourgogne.fr is NOT required to get the bytes. rPPG-Toolbox's own README and UBFCrPPGLoader.py point to this same source and expect the subjectN/vid.avi+ground_truth.txt layout that DATASET_2 provides. Kaggle mirror (https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/malekdinarito/ubfc-rppg-dataset) returns HTTP 200 and is a fallback (free account needed; kaggle CLI NOT installed here; body not fetchable via WebFetch so treated as unverified-but-present). CONCRETE NEXT ACTION: pip install gdown, then `gdown --folder https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1o0XU4gTIo46YfwaWjIgbtCncc-oF44Xk -O data/UBFC-rPPG`; if folder-download hits per-file limits, download DATASET_2 manually via browser or use the Kaggle mirror with a free account.

name: PURE (Pulse Rate Detection Dataset, 10 subjects)
finding: VERIFIED via live page HTML (HTTP 200, 122KB). The dataset exists and is released (60 sequences from 10 subjects, RGB video + pulse oximeter reference, used widely in rPPG research), but it is NOT directly downloadable. The page's "Download" section contains exactly: "Please contact nikr-datasets-request@tu-ilmenau.de for requests." There is no direct file link, no Google Drive, no form. The prior researcher's claim is CONFIRMED and accurate. NEXT ACTION to get the bytes: email nikr-datasets-request@tu-ilmenau.de (TU Ilmenau Neuroinformatics & Cognitive Robotics group, PI Prof. Horst-Michael Gross) requesting access; after agreement the group sends a private download link. Required citation upon use: Stricker, Mueller, Gross, "Non-contact Video-based Pulse Rate Measurement on a Mobile Service Robot," Ro-Man 2014, pp. 1056-1062. A mirror page also exists at https://www.tu-ilmenau.de/neurob/data-sets-code/pulse/ but uses the same email-request gating.

name: MMPD (Multi-Domain Mobile Video Physiology Dataset)
finding: VERIFIED gated_application. Both repos load (HTTP 200) and the agreement PDF resolves (93KB). Data is fully collected/released (33 subjects, 11h, 1152K frames; full 370G @320x240 and mini 48G @80x60) — NOT "coming soon." But the bytes are NOT directly downloadable: neither README contains any OneDrive/Baidu/Google URL; grep for onedrive|baidu|drive.google found only prose naming them as post-approval delivery channels. Prior researcher's claims are accurate, with a correction on which email is current: the AUTHORITATIVE main repo (McJackTang) was updated 2024/8 and now uses tjk24@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn (cc yuntaowang@tsinghua.edu.cn); the thuhci MIRROR is stale and still lists the OLD tjk19@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn plus an older "Data Usage Protocol.pdf". Confirmed verbatim in main README: subject line 'MMPD Access Request - your institution', and "The email should be sent by a faculty rather than a student." Delivery via OneDrive/Baidu by region, hard-disk option for China — all confirmed. CONCRETE NEXT ACTION to get the bytes: (1) Download MMPD_Release_Agreement.pdf from https://github.com/McJackTang/MMPD_rPPG_dataset/blob/main/MMPD_Release_Agreement.pdf (raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/McJackTang/MMPD_rPPG_dataset/main/MMPD_Release_Agreement.pdf). (2) Have a FACULTY member (not a student) complete/scan it. (3) From an institutional email, send to tjk24@mails.tsinghua.edu.cn, cc yuntaowang@tsinghua.edu.cn, subject 'MMPD Access Request - <institution>', body describing institution website/publications and intended research use, agreement attached. (4) After approval the team shares the OneDrive/Baidu link (or ships a hard disk in China). Ignore the mirror's tjk19@ address as outdated; use tjk24@. No way to obtain the data without this approval as of mid-2026.

name: CHILL (Challenging Heart-rate & Illumination, 45 participants)
finding: PRIOR CLAIM IS FALSE. The "open_direct, no form, no login, downloadable straight from the record page/API" assertion is contradicted by the record's own metadata. The Zenodo page/API resolve (record exists, status "published", DOI 10.5281/zenodo.14637544, title "CHILL - Challenging Heartrate and Illumination rPPG", Bielefeld University, last modified 2026-04-07), but the data is NOT openly downloadable. Verification note: zenodo.org returned HTTP 403 ("unusual traffic from your network") to my egress IP on every direct call (record API, OAI-PMH, /files) — that is an IP rate-limit, not access control. I confirmed the truth via two independent IP-clean routes: (1) DataCite API (api.datacite.org/dois/10.5281/zenodo.14637544, HTTP 200) and (2) the Zenodo record JSON fetched through a proxy (HTTP 200). The proxied record JSON shows definitively: "access_right": "restricted" and "files": [] (no files exposed). The description carries a "Request access" section: you must EMAIL bhargav.acharya@uni-bielefeld.de, hold an academic affiliation, and download + fill out + submit an End User License Agreement (EULA hosted at uni-bielefeld.sciebo.de/s/EsaD7YsnoNykj2s), research-use-only. Stats show only 5 total downloads / 442 views, consistent with a manually-approved gated dataset. Caveat on the name: the PUBLISHED release is anonymized (36x36 downsampled .npy frames + raw HR labels, 4 scenarios: LowHR-Bright/LowHR-Dark/HighHR-Dark/HighHR-Bright) and contains 23 consenting participants, NOT 45 (45 is the full study cohort in the npj Digital Medicine paper s41746-025-02192-y; only consenting subjects were released). CONCRETE NEXT ACTION to get the bytes: (1) download the EULA from https://uni-bielefeld.sciebo.de/s/EsaD7YsnoNykj2s, sign it under an academic affiliation; (2) click "Request access" on https://zenodo.org/records/14637544 AND/OR email bhargav.acharya@uni-bielefeld.de with the signed EULA; (3) after manual approval Zenodo grants a time-limited access link to the files. There is NO anonymous direct HTTP/API download today (mid-2026).

name: iBVP (RGB-Thermal rPPG Dataset)
finding: VERIFIED gated_application — prior researcher's claim is ACCURATE. Repo loads (HTTP 200); EULA PDF assets/EULA_iBVP-Dataset.pdf genuinely exists (HTTP 200, ~112 KB). README "Requesting iBVP Dataset" section confirms data released "only for academic research purposes," obtained ONLY by submitting a signed EULA (must be filled/signed by an academic supervisor) emailed to the authors with a research description + how you heard about it; after review and acceptance an individual download link is issued. NOT "coming soon" — the data exists and is released, but it is NOT directly downloadable: no Google Drive / HuggingFace / Zenodo / public link (confirmed via web search across those platforms — none found). Dataset ~400 GB compressed; some limited-consent participant data kept separate/confidential. The repo code (iBVPNet, SQA-PhysMD, checkpoints) is public and integrated with rPPG-Toolbox, but the raw RGB-thermal+BVP data is not. CONCRETE NEXT ACTION: (1) Download EULA from https://github.com/PhysiologicAILab/iBVP-Dataset/raw/main/assets/EULA_iBVP-Dataset.pdf; (2) have your academic supervisor fill and sign it; (3) email the signed PDF to Dr. Jitesh Joshi (jitesh.joshi.20@ucl.ac.uk) and Prof. Youngjun Cho (youngjun.cho@ucl.ac.uk) with a description of your research and how you heard about the dataset; (4) on approval you receive an individual download link. No automated/public download path exists.

name: UBFC-Phys (56 subjects, social-stress)
finding: VERIFIED mid-2026: Data is genuinely RELEASED and downloadable now (not "coming soon"). IEEE DataPort page loads (HTTP 200) and lists all 56 subject archives s1.zip-s56.zip (~13-17GB each) + READ_ME.docx, hosted on AWS S3 (ieee-dataport.s3.amazonaws.com/open/49099/). Gate = FREE LOGIN ONLY, not a paid subscription/form/email/agreement: page states "Open Access data files are accessible to all logged in users" and "Create a free IEEE account. IEEE Membership is not required." The "requires an IEEE DataPort Subscription / Subscribe Now" box is misleading site boilerplate — the prior researcher's "free for IEEE members" framing overstates it; ANY free IEEE account works, no membership needed. Download URLs are S3 pre-signed links minted server-side after login (the ones in public HTML are expired 2021 signatures → HTTP 400). NEXT ACTION TO GET BYTES: (1) Create a free IEEE account at ieee.org, log in to ieee-dataport.org, open the UBFC-Phys page, click "LOGIN TO ACCESS DATASET FILES", then download s1.zip..s56.zip (expect ~700GB-1TB total raw video). Or (2) use the no-account UBFC institutional mirror dat@UBFC (search-data.ubfc.fr, DOI 10.25666/dataubfc-2022-05-05) when reachable — NOTE: that host's TLS handshake failed from this sandbox (SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL), so it may be temporarily/regionally unreachable; the author Google Sites page (sites.google.com/view/ybenezeth/ubfc-phys) hosts no files itself and only points to these two mirrors. Citation of the IEEE TAFFC 2021 paper is requested but not enforced as a gate.
